vision_report.temporal_deltas: From EARLIEST to T-2, the pots remained static with no visible plant growth. T-1 and CURRENT images are completely black, indicating a total loss of visual data or lighting failure.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. There is no evidence of the registered species (String of Nickels, Mexican Mint, Pothos, Silver Guest). The environment is effectively a collection of empty pots.
vision_report.anomalies: The transition to total darkness in the final two frames suggests a hardware failure or a complete lack of ambient light in the observation zone.
vision_report.narrative_description: I have performed a multi-stage audit of the provided image sequence. Initially, the pots were visible but contained no discernible plant life, only soil and minor debris. The white rabbit anchor was present in the P3/P4 area. As the sequence progressed, the visual data degraded into total darkness. I conclude that the botanical specimens are either absent, deceased, or the observation system has suffered a critical lighting failure, rendering the biome unobservable.
